# The Fog Break

> Peer-reviewed golf physics, written in plain English, with explicit citations.
> We translate research on equipment and technique into honest, forensic
> explanations — what the gear actually does at impact, what it doesn't, and
> where the marketing stops matching the math.

The Fog Break is a golf physics publication for players who want to understand
the science behind their equipment and swing. Every article is grounded in
peer-reviewed mechanics, worked through from first principles, and paired with
interactive tools. Citations are explicit and linked. When a claim has a number
on it, the paper it came from is named.

## Published articles

- [The Friction Machine — Wedge Backspin Physics](https://thefogbreak.com/articles/wedge-backspin.html): Issue No. III. Grooves do not generate backspin — friction does. A forensic examination of oblique impact mechanics at the wedge-ball interface. Covers: normal force vs. tangential force, effective friction coefficient (μ), groove cross-section geometry (V vs. U vs. post-2010 conforming), the backspin maximum at 54–56° loft and why it falls beyond 60°, urethane vs. ionomer cover deformation physics (~70% spin gap), moisture's effect on μ (avg. 35% dry-spin loss, face moisture worse than ball moisture), the flier lie mechanism, and the 2010 USGA groove rule (edge radius ≥0.010", volume formula). Concludes with four variables ranked by practical impact: (1) ball cover, (2) face cleanliness, (3) lie quality, (4) angle of attack. 13 references. 18-minute read, 3 figures.

- [Launch Angle, Spin Loft, and the 15 Yards You're Leaving on the Tee](https://thefogbreak.com/articles/driver-launch-v2.html): Issue No. II. Spin loft — the angular gap between dynamic loft and angle of attack — is the single variable governing driver distance efficiency. Covers: spin loft formula (Spin Loft = Dynamic Loft − Angle of Attack), smash factor relationship, PGA Tour average data (113 mph / −1.3° AoA / 2,686 rpm / 275 yd carry), optimal launch windows by swing speed, why most amateurs hit down on the driver (−3° to −5° AoA), and practical setup adjustments. 11 references. 16-minute read, 3 figures.

- [What Your Putter Actually Does and Does Not Do at Impact](https://thefogbreak.com/articles/putter-physics.html): Issue No. I. A forensic look at blade, mallet, and zero-torque putters through oblique-impact mechanics. Key findings: face angle contributes 83–92% of initial ball direction (not path); MOI determines twist resistance on off-center strikes but has a practical ceiling; horizontal gear effect (Lambeth 2020) causes mis-hit balls to curve back toward the intended line; the dimpled ball's aerodynamic properties put a ceiling on all putter designs. 18-minute read, 5 figures.

## Interactive tools

- [Driver Spin Loft Calculator](https://thefogbreak.com/tools/driver-spin-loft.html): Input dynamic loft and angle of attack to calculate spin loft, predicted spin rate, smash factor, and optimal launch window. Built from the equations in Issue No. II.

- [Putter Face Angle Visualizer](https://thefogbreak.com/tools/putter-face-angle.html): Adjust face angle, putt distance, putter type, and strike location. Returns predicted miss accounting for face-angle contribution, horizontal gear effect, and impact-ratio roll-out behavior. Built from the equations in Issue No. I.

## Index

- [Homepage](https://thefogbreak.com/): Latest issue and archive.
- [Tools index](https://thefogbreak.com/tools/): All interactive physics tools.

## Editorial standards

- Every physical claim is sourced. We link to the paper or we don't make the claim.
- We favor mechanics over brand names. Where we name a model, we name it because the design illustrates a principle, not because we endorse it.
- We publish the equations. If the math doesn't support the story, the story changes.
- Data presented as PGA Tour averages is sourced from TrackMan. Controlled impact research is cited to specific peer-reviewed papers.

## Publication

The Fog Break publishes weekly from Boulder, Colorado. It is an independent publication by Fog + Flag (fogandflag.com).
